text:
CT CHEST / ABDOMEN / PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST
ANNUAL POST-TRANSPLANT SURVEILLANCE STUDY

CLINICAL INDICATION:
Renata Vasquez-Okonkwo, 49-year-old female with history of acute myeloid leukemia (AML), FAB M1, FLT3-ITD high allelic ratio, trisomy 8, ELN Adverse risk, diagnosed approximately 15 months ago. She underwent induction chemotherapy with 7+3 plus midostaurin and achieved complete remission with MRD negativity after first induction. Subsequently underwent myeloablative conditioning allogeneic peripheral blood stem cell transplantation from a 10/10 HLA-matched unrelated donor (fludarabine/busulfan conditioning) approximately 12 months ago. Presenting today for annual surveillance CT as part of post-transplant monitoring protocol. Most recent bone marrow biopsy (day +365) demonstrated MRD-negative complete remission with full donor chimerism. Currently off all immunosuppression. No new clinical symptoms reported.

TECHNIQUE:
Multidetector CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis performed with intravenous contrast (iohexol 350 mgI/mL, 100 mL injected at 3 mL/sec via antecubital IV). Acquisitions obtained in the portal venous phase for abdomen and pelvis, and in the standard chest protocol. Multiplanar reformations in the coronal and sagittal planes were generated and reviewed. Comparison made to prior CT chest/abdomen/pelvis performed at 6 months post-transplant.

FINDINGS:

CHEST:

Lungs and Airways: The lungs are clear. No pulmonary nodules, consolidation, or ground-glass opacities are identified. The previously documented right lower lobe ground-glass opacity (noted on day +90 CT, resolved on 6-month CT) remains absent. The airways are patent to the segmental level bilaterally. No endobronchial lesion. No bronchiectasis.

Pleura: No pleural effusions bilaterally. No pleural thickening. No pneumothorax.

Mediastinum: The mediastinal contour is normal. Heart is normal in size with no pericardial effusion. The main pulmonary artery is not enlarged. Aorta is normal in caliber throughout its thoracic course.

Lymph Nodes: No enlarged lymph nodes in the prevascular, paratracheal, aortopulmonary window, subcarinal, or hilar regions. No lymph node is enlarged by size criteria. No abnormal morphology or confluent nodal masses.

Chest Wall / Osseous Structures: Visualized ribs, thoracic vertebrae, and clavicles are intact without lytic or sclerotic foci. No suspicious osseous lesions identified. No soft tissue mass in the chest wall.

ABDOMEN:

Liver: The liver is normal in size with a craniocaudal span of approximately 13.2 cm (previously enlarged at 17.1 cm on initial presentation; normal at prior surveillance scans). Hepatic parenchyma enhances homogeneously without focal hypo- or hyperdense lesions. No intrahepatic biliary ductal dilatation. Portal and hepatic veins are patent. No ascites in the perihepatic space.

Spleen: The spleen is normal in size, measuring approximately 9.5 cm in greatest craniocaudal dimension (previously 16.4 cm at initial presentation; 10.8 cm at pre-transplant CT; 9.8 cm at 6-month CT). Splenic parenchyma is homogeneous without focal lesion. No splenic infarct. The spleen has returned to and maintained normal dimensions throughout the post-transplant course, consistent with resolution of the leukemia-associated splenomegaly.

Pancreas: The pancreas is of normal size, contour, and attenuation throughout the head, neck, body, and tail. Pancreatic duct is not dilated. No peripancreatic stranding or fluid collection.

Kidneys and Adrenal Glands: Both kidneys are normal in size with smooth cortical contour and symmetric nephrographic enhancement. No renal mass. No hydronephrosis. No nephrolithiasis identified. Bilateral adrenal glands are normal in size and configuration without nodularity.

Gallbladder and Bile Ducts: The gallbladder is present, without stones, wall thickening, or pericholecystic fluid. Common bile duct is not dilated (3 mm).

Bowel: Visualized loops of small and large bowel are of normal caliber with no wall thickening or abnormal enhancement. No bowel obstruction. No free intraperitoneal air.

Vascular Structures: The abdominal aorta is normal in caliber. The celiac axis, superior mesenteric artery, and inferior mesenteric artery origins appear patent. No vascular anomaly identified.

Lymph Nodes: No enlarged lymph nodes in the upper abdominal, retroperitoneal, mesenteric, or porta hepatis regions. No nodal conglomerate mass. No soft tissue density lesion in the retroperitoneum to suggest extramedullary hematopoietic or leukemic involvement.

Soft Tissues / Peritoneum: No peritoneal implants or omental thickening. No ascites. No soft tissue mass or collection in the abdomen.

PELVIS:

Lymph Nodes: No enlarged pelvic or inguinal lymph nodes. No iliac chain adenopathy.

Bladder: The urinary bladder is partially distended and appears normal without wall thickening or filling defect.

Gynecologic Structures: The uterus and adnexa appear unremarkable. No pelvic free fluid. No adnexal mass.

Osseous Structures: The visualized lumbar spine, sacrum, iliac wings, and proximal femora demonstrate no lytic lesions, sclerotic foci, or cortical destruction. No pathologic fracture. No soft tissue mass arising from or adjacent to the osseous pelvis. The pelvic bones are intact. There is no evidence of osseous involvement by extramedullary leukemia.

IMPRESSION:

1. No evidence of lymphadenopathy above or below the diaphragm. No nodal masses or soft tissue density lesions to suggest extramedullary AML, myeloid sarcoma, or chloroma at any imaged site.

2. The lungs are clear. No pulmonary nodules, infiltrates, or masses. The previously documented right lower lobe ground-glass opacity has been resolved on prior imaging and remains absent.

3. The liver is normal in size and appearance. No hepatic lesions. Complete resolution of the previously documented hepatomegaly.

4. The spleen is normal in size (9.5 cm), maintaining the interval improvement seen since initial presentation. Splenic parenchyma is homogeneous without focal abnormality. Complete and sustained resolution of the original leukemia-associated splenomegaly.

5. No pleural effusions, pericardial effusion, or ascites.

6. No evidence of extramedullary leukemic involvement, including no discrete soft tissue masses in the retroperitoneum, pelvis, or osseous structures that would raise concern for myeloid sarcoma.

7. Unremarkable post-transplant CT appearance overall, without radiographically apparent disease recurrence or new pathologic findings.

Radiologist attestation: I personally reviewed all images and the report reflects my independent interpretation.

Attending Radiologist: [Electronically signed]
